title: solidago arguta subsp. caroliniana
intro: Solidago arguta subsp. caroliniana is a subspecies of the goldenrod plant, specifically a type of shrubby goldenrod that is native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Solidago arguta subsp. caroliniana include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in topical creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
